priority.when: Use first to inspect widgets, packaging scripts, and expected output locations
priority.why: Packaging decisions depend on understanding the current project layout

priority.when: Use for toolkit configuration, packaging scripts, and widget metadata files
priority.why: Reading current inputs avoids packaging the wrong widget set or changing the wrong files

priority.when: Use for targeted edits to packaging configuration or packaging source files
priority.why: Packaging changes should stay minimal and precise

priority.when: Use to run packaging and related validation commands
priority.why: Packaging is command-driven and should be observable from command output

priority.when: Use only for explicit BAW deployment or BAW environment inspection requests
priority.why: MCP operations can have external effects and should remain deliberate

best_practices.practice: Inspect widgets/ and packaging-related files before editing configuration
Use list results to confirm the requested widget names exist
Read toolkit.config.json and the selected packaging entry points before changing them
Read enough surrounding context to understand how widget selection is represented
Make small, targeted updates to packaging configuration and script files
Avoid broad refactors during routine packaging tasks
Use for packaging execution and lightweight validation checks
Always specify template version: python3 package_baw.py
Capture output that helps confirm success, warnings, or blocking failures
Use only after the user has explicitly asked for deployment or BAW-side inspection
Report asynchronous status clearly when the MCP operation returns a queued job

boundary.rule: Do not make widget implementation changes unless the packaging issue explicitly requires a mode handoff
Keep deployment actions separate from ordinary packaging tasks unless the user requests them
